retry_reason: The refcount initialization itself never fails; the only failure mode is memory allocation failure, which is a generic resource exhaustion — the caller may or may not retry on that, but the refcount part is not retry-specific.
contract_summary: Set wrapper that allocates a new iommufd object of the specified type and initializes its embedded reference count(s) (obj.users.refs and obj.wait_cnt.refs) to their starting values (typically 1). The caller receives a pointer to the allocated object with an initial reference. The caller must later call iommufd_object_finalize() to make the object visible to other users, or iommufd_object_abort_and_destroy() on error to free it. Ownership of this initial reference is transferred to the caller, who must release it with a corresponding put when the object is no longer needed.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to allocate a typed iommufd object and initialize its reference count (via _iommufd_object_alloc). The only additional logic is a compile-time offset check (BUILD_BUG_ON_ZERO), which is negligible.

check.reason: The extracted access paths (e.g., "obj.users.refs" with 3 segments) and type chains (e.g., "iommufd_ioas--iommufd_object--refcount_t--atomic_t" with 4 types) have mismatched segment counts (3 != 4), and the type chains include an extra type (iommufd_ioas) not present in the callee information. Additionally, the callee information specifies type chains starting from iommufd_object, not from iommufd_ioas, so the candidate paths are inconsistent with the callee's description.

reevaluate.reason: The macro calls _iommufd_object_alloc, which initializes both membership reflectors (obj.users.refs and obj.wait_cnt.refs) to 1. The macro itself is a thin wrapper that casts the raw iommufd_object pointer to the container type (e.g., iommufd_ioas) via container_of. The candidate operations correctly identify the set operations on the two refcounts, with the type chain built from the container type (iommufd_ioas) down to atomic_t. The check stage's rejection based on segment count mismatch is a technicality; the operations are semantically correct and the function is a pure refcount initialization wrapper.

final.retry_reason: The refcount initialization itself never fails; the only failure mode is memory allocation failure, which is a generic resource exhaustion — the caller may or may not retry on that, but the refcount part is not retry-specific.
